name: Mendenhall Glacier Helicopter Tour & Trek
description: Soar over the Juneau Icefield and land on Mendenhall Glacier for a guided trek across ancient blue ice. Includes hiking boots, rain gear, and experienced glacier guide. One of Alaska's most iconic experiences.

name: Juneau Whale Watching & Wildlife Cruise
description: Board a small group catamaran for a whale watching expedition in the whale-rich waters of Stephens Passage. Virtually guaranteed humpback whale sightings May through September. May also spot orcas, sea lions, eagles, and bears along the coastline.

image.alt: Humpback whale breaching in Juneau waters

name: Glacier Dog Sledding Experience
description: Fly by helicopter to a glacier camp and ride a dog sled team across pristine glacier ice. Meet Alaskan Huskies, learn mushing commands, and experience the thrill of this quintessential Alaska adventure. Suitable for all ages.

image.alt: Dog sled team on glacier near Juneau

name: Denali National Park Guided Wilderness Hike
description: Join a certified naturalist for a full-day guided hike into the Denali backcountry. Traverse tundra, cross glacial streams, and spot wildlife including grizzly bears, caribou, and Dall sheep with the towering peak of Denali as your backdrop.

image.alt: Hikers on trail with Denali mountain in background

name: Misty Fjords Floatplane Adventure
description: Fly by floatplane over the breathtaking Misty Fjords National Monument, a wilderness of towering granite cliffs, cascading waterfalls, and pristine alpine lakes. Land on a remote lake for a short nature walk before flying back.

image.alt: Floatplane over Misty Fjords National Monument

name: Katmai Bear Viewing Day Trip
description: Fly from Anchorage to Katmai National Park for a day of brown bear viewing at one of the world's premier bear-watching locations. Watch bears fishing for salmon at Brooks Falls during the annual salmon run. Includes bush plane flight, park entry, and guide.

image.alt: Brown bears fishing at Brooks Falls in Katmai

name: Kenai Fjords Sea Kayaking
description: Paddle through the pristine waters of Kenai Fjords National Park, navigating among icebergs calved from tidewater glaciers. Spot sea otters, puffins, and harbor seals while your guide shares insights about the fjord ecosystem.

image.alt: Kayakers paddling near glacier in Kenai Fjords

packingRecommendations: Water shoes or sandals with straps

name: White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad
description: Ride the historic narrow-gauge railway from Skagway through dramatic mountain scenery, climbing nearly 3,000 feet in just 20 miles. Built during the 1898 Klondike Gold Rush, this engineering marvel offers breathtaking views of waterfalls, tunnels, and trestle bridges.

image.alt: White Pass railroad train on mountain tracks

name: Kenai River Salmon Fishing Charter
description: Fish for world-class King and Silver salmon on the legendary Kenai River with an experienced local guide. All gear, tackle, and fishing licenses included. Fish cleaning and vacuum packing available for your catch.

image.alt: Angler holding King salmon on Kenai River

name: Northern Lights Viewing Tour (Fairbanks)
description: Travel to a prime aurora viewing location outside Fairbanks, away from city lights. Enjoy hot drinks in a heated viewing lodge while waiting for the aurora borealis. Photography tips included. Best viewed late August through early April.
packingRecommendations: Very warm layers (temperatures can drop below freezing)

name: Kenai Fjords National Park Glacier Cruise
description: Full-day cruise through Kenai Fjords National Park visiting Aialik Glacier and the Holgate Arm. See tidewater glaciers calving into the sea, spot whales, puffins, sea otters, and sea lions. Lunch included aboard.

image.alt: Cruise boat near Aialik Glacier in Kenai Fjords

name: Icy Strait Point ZipRider
description: Experience the world's largest zipline at Icy Strait Point, soaring 1,300 feet above the forest floor at speeds up to 60 mph with panoramic views of Icy Strait and the surrounding mountains. The ride covers 5,330 feet in about 90 seconds.

image.alt: ZipRider zipline at Icy Strait Point
